tasks.engine: {{ENGINE — e.g. opencode, the harness engine that reads the task model field}}
tasks.spec: You are running one cell of a model bakeoff for {{PRODUCT}}. Boundary: this cell owns only the session directory '{{WORKDIR}}/{{MODEL_KEY}}--{{SCENARIO_KEY}}' and the file evaluator-notes.md inside it. Do not modify product code, harness files, prompts, engine config, or another cell's session. The candidate model for this cell is {{CANDIDATE_MODEL — e.g. openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2}}; the manifest's per-task model field is the source of truth, not a hard-coded engine block. SCENARIO: {{SCENARIO — the persona, prompt, or task; keep wording identical for every model in this scenario row}}. HOW TO RUN: drive the real product with {{HARNESS_COMMAND_WITH_MODEL — exact command that runs this scenario against the manifest model and writes to the session dir}}. Use the same scenario wording and the same grading contract for every model. Do not adapt the scenario to make one model look better. If the harness fails, record the failure as the result. OUTPUT CONTRACT for evaluator-notes.md: include '# Bakeoff Cell', 'MODEL: <candidate model>', 'SCENARIO: <scenario key>', '## Criteria Grades', '## Evidence', '## Failures Or Costs', and a final line exactly shaped 'VERDICT: <score or pass/fail summary>'. Grade {{OUTPUT_CONTRACT — same numbered criteria for every cell}} with PASS, FAIL, or MIXED and cite transcript evidence. Keep the notes under 1000 words. Hard rules: no invented facts, mark assumptions, and verify the run used the candidate model before claiming a result.
tasks.check: /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.14 '{{CHECK_SCRIPT_PATH — absolute path to templates/bakeoff/checks/bakeoff.py}}' --session-dir '{{WORKDIR}}/{{MODEL_KEY}}--{{SCENARIO_KEY}}' --notes '{{WORKDIR}}/{{MODEL_KEY}}--{{SCENARIO_KEY}}/evaluator-notes.md' --expected-model '{{CANDIDATE_MODEL — e.g. openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.2}}' --session-validator '{{SESSION_VALIDATOR — exact command that proves this session ran with the expected model}}'
tasks.verified: the notes name the candidate model, end with a verdict, and the session validator confirmed that the expected model ran the scenario
